content.problemStatement:
You are given a graph GGG with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM edges. You should partition the vertices of GGG into two sets AAA and BBB such that:


each vertex of GGG belongs to exactly one of these sets
AAA is non-empty
AAA is an independent set in GGG, i.e. for each pair of vertices u,v∈Au, v \in Au,v∈A, GGG does not contain an edge (u,v)(u, v)(u,v)
for each vertex a∈Aa \in Aa∈A and each vertex b∈Bb \in Bb∈B, there is an edge (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) in GGG
Find the number of such partitions (A,B)(A, B)(A,B). Also, give an example of one of these partitions or determine that no such partition exists.

Two partitions are considered different if there is a vertex that is in the set AAA in one partition and in the set BBB in the other partition.
constraints: there are no self-loops or duplicate edges in the graph
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
Each of the next MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that vertices uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print two lines.


The first of these lines should contain a single integer — the number of partitions satisfying the given conditions.
The second line should contain a single string with length NNN. If there are no partitions satisfying the given conditions, each character of this string should be '0'. Otherwise, this string should describe one such partition — for each valid iii, the iii-th character should be '1' if vertex iii belongs to AAA or '0' if it belongs to BBB.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The only possible partition satisfying the given conditions is A={1,2},B={3,4,5}A = \{1, 2\}, B = \{3, 4, 5\}A={1,2},B={3,4,5}.
